sources.label: StockInvest.us RDX.AX snapshot (price action, 52-week range, consensus target A$3.64, next earnings Aug 26 2026)
Redox announcements archive (latest items: Garlicin product launch Jun 1, Sucralose May 27 - no material corporate news)
Redox acquires Molekulis (transformer/specialty oils) - dated April/May 2025, not new
Redox S&P/ASX 300 inclusion - dated March 2024, rules out fresh index-flow catalyst

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent-alpha lens: +4.9% today to a fresh 52-week high (A$3.67 vs prior high A$3.52), ~+18% in two weeks and +19.5% YTD with zero new evidence on the speculative coolants/water-treatment pathway; the stock now trades above the A$3.64 consensus target, so residual alpha has compressed versus yesterday's hold.
verdicts.bull: Diversified ANZ chemical distributor with real earnings growth (1H26 revenue +6.6%, NPAT +8.9%) and an existing transformer-oils line via Molekulis that gives it genuine, if small, exposure to grid/data-centre electrification chemistry.
verdicts.neutral: Nothing material has changed since June 6; the AI-chain pathway remains a qualitative 'could supply' option, and the two-week melt-up looks like momentum and small-cap flow rather than any fundamental re-rating of that option.
verdicts.bear: A US$1.3B distributor with no disclosed AI/data-centre contracts has rerated ~20% YTD to above consensus targets; if the latent thesis never converts, the position is now paying chase risk for a commodity-distribution multiple.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: FY26 results on Aug 26, 2026, or any disclosed coolant/water-treatment/transformer-oil supply agreement tied to data-centre or grid customers.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Crowding/momentum reversal: the stock has run ahead of analyst targets on no news, so a flow unwind retraces the move with the latent thesis no closer to validation.
